title: Tighten urlKey tracking-param regex so fbclid/gclid don't over-match prefixes
problem: model.js:35 uses /^(utm_|fbclid|gclid|ref$)/i. The alternatives fbclid and gclid are bare prefixes, so real query params like fbclidx or gclide are incorrectly stripped (both .test() true), collapsing distinct URLs onto one canonical key and producing false duplicates. Only ref is correctly exact-anchored.
proposed_change: Anchor the exact-match params: /^(utm_|fbclid$|gclid$|ref$)/i (keep utm_ as a prefix since UTM params are a family). No change for utm_*, fbclid, gclid, ref.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js urlKey edge cases: expect(urlKey('https://e.com/?fbclidx=1')).toBe('e.com/?fbclidx=1') and expect(urlKey('https://e.com/?fbclid=1')).toBe('e.com/'); same for gclid/gclide.
rationale: Real correctness bug affecting the core dedup primitive; tiny diff, fully unit-testable, and a regression test locks it in. Highest value/lowest risk so it goes first.

title: Guard escapeHtml against non-string titles in exportHtml
problem: bookmarks.js:227 escapeHtml = (s) => s.replace(...) throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading replace)' when s is undefined/null. exportHtml (line 223) passes b.title straight in; exportHtml accepts an arbitrary items array, so a title-less item crashes the entire export.
proposed_change: Coerce in escapeHtml: const escapeHtml = (s) => String(s ?? '').replace(...). Identical output for all current string inputs.
test_plan: In test/bookmarks.test.js exportHtml describe, add a case with { url, title: undefined, tags: [], dateAdded: 1000 } and assert it does not throw and emits an empty title segment.
rationale: Prevents a crash that takes down the whole export; one-line hardening with byte-identical normal output.

title: Harden exportHtml against missing dateAdded (NaN ADD_DATE)
problem: bookmarks.js:223 computes ADD_DATE="${Math.floor(b.dateAdded / 1e3)}". When a bookmark passed to exportHtml lacks dateAdded (an item that did not flow through walk, the only place that defaults it via n.dateAdded || Date.now()), Math.floor(undefined/1e3) yields NaN, emitting ADD_DATE="NaN" into the Netscape HTML.
proposed_change: Default the timestamp at the call site: ADD_DATE="${Math.floor((b.dateAdded || Date.now()) / 1e3)}". No behavior change for bookmarks that already carry dateAdded.
test_plan: In test/bookmarks.test.js exportHtml describe, add a case exporting an item with no dateAdded and assert output contains a numeric ADD_DATE value and NOT ADD_DATE="NaN".
rationale: Produces always-valid Netscape HTML; refines current behavior without changing the normal path. (Merges the two duplicate NaN-ADD_DATE candidates.)

title: Escape URL and tags when emitting Netscape bookmark HTML
problem: exportHtml (bookmarks.js:222-227) interpolates b.url and b.tags.join(',') directly into HREF="..." and TAGS="..." attributes without escaping; only b.title runs through escapeHtml. A URL or tag containing a double-quote breaks out of the attribute, and the exported .html is a real browser-openable document, so attribute-injection payloads survive a round-trip.
proposed_change: Wrap the HREF and TAGS values in the existing escapeHtml helper: HREF="${escapeHtml(b.url)}" and TAGS="${escapeHtml(b.tags.join(','))}". No new helper; escapeHtml is a no-op on characters other than &<>".
test_plan: In test/bookmarks.test.js exportHtml describe add a bookmark with url containing "> and a tag t">x; assert output does NOT contain the raw ">
rationale: Closes an attribute-injection sink with the helper already applied to the title; identical output for well-formed URLs/tags.

title: Make hostOf strip www case-insensitively for consistency with urlKey
problem: hostOf (bookmarks.js:4) strips ^www\. case-sensitively, while urlKey (model.js:36) lowercases the host before stripping www. A URL like https://WWW.Example.com yields domain WWW.Example.com from hostOf but key example.com/... from urlKey, so suggestion-dedup byDomain grouping and domainColor keying can diverge from the canonical key for the same site.
proposed_change: Lowercase before stripping in hostOf: new URL(url).hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/^www\./, ''). Matches urlKey's host normalization; no change for already-lowercase hosts.
test_plan: Add a unit test (export hostOf or assert via loadEnriched) with an uppercase WWW. host and assert the resulting domain is lowercased and www-stripped, equal to the host portion of urlKey for the same URL.
rationale: Aligns the two host-normalization paths without changing the common lowercase-host case.

title: Make domainColor deterministic for empty/non-string domain
problem: model.js:19-24 domainColor(domain) iterates domain.length; a non-string (undefined) throws on .length/.charCodeAt. Favicon fallback (Favicon.jsx:28 background: b.color) relies on this color; an empty string yields hue 0 (valid) but the undefined/empty contract is not pinned.
proposed_change: Coerce defensively at the top: domain = domain || ''; (one line). Output for every real domain string is unchanged; empty/undefined now deterministically returns the hue-0 color instead of throwing.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js: domainColor('') returns hsl(0 55% 45%); domainColor(undefined) returns the same and does not throw; domainColor('github.com') unchanged from current value.
rationale: Hardens an exported pure helper against the inputs the fallback path can hit, with byte-identical output for non-empty domains.

title: Guard Favicon initial against missing domain via a pure initial() helper
problem: Favicon.jsx:29 computes (b.domain[0] || '?').toUpperCase(). The || '?' only rescues an empty-string domain; if b.domain is undefined/null, b.domain[0] throws a TypeError and crashes the row render. Other code (byDomain uses a.b.domain || '') treats domain as possibly-absent.
proposed_change: Extract export const initial = (domain) => ((domain || '')[0] || '?').toUpperCase(); in model.js and use it in Favicon.jsx as {initial(b.domain)}. No behavior change for the non-empty domain path.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js describe('initial'): initial('github.com')->'G', initial('')->'?', initial(undefined)->'?', initial(null)->'?'.
rationale: Removes a row-render crash while preserving exact output; the extracted pure function is unit-testable in node.

title: Make setMeta not mutate the caller's meta object
problem: store.js:48 does meta._ts = Date.now() directly on the argument. patchMeta passes a fresh clone so it is safe there, but setMeta is exported and used directly (e.g. demo/seed path), so direct callers have their object silently stamped, which is inconsistent with the pure apply* transformers.
proposed_change: Stamp on a shallow copy: const out = { ...meta, _ts: Date.now() }; use out for the writes and the returned { meta: out, oversize }. Persisted data identical.
test_plan: In test/store.test.js setMeta describe, pass const input = { pinned: ['a'] }, call setMeta, assert input._ts === undefined while returned meta._ts === NOW. Existing setMeta tests still pass.
rationale: Removes a hidden side effect on caller-owned data while keeping stored output identical. (Merges the two duplicate setMeta-mutation candidates.)

title: Coerce ids to String once in applyRestore/applyUnarchive
problem: applyRestore (store.js:135) and applyUnarchive (store.js:147) call ids.map(String) inside the filter predicate, recomputing the mapped array for every element (O(n*m)). applyTrash/applyArchive correctly map once, so this is an inconsistency between paired transformers.
proposed_change: Hoist the mapping: const sids = ids.map(String); once at the top of applyRestore and applyUnarchive, then filter against !sids.includes(x). Identical results.
test_plan: Existing trash/restore and archive/unarchive round-trip tests in test/store-meta.test.js cover the behavior and must still pass. Optionally assert restore with numeric ids ([1]) drops the string-stored id '1'.
rationale: Aligns remove-transformers with their add counterparts and removes redundant per-element allocation; output unchanged and covered by existing tests.

title: selectBookmarks: hoist the duplicated stable-sort spread into a helper
problem: selectBookmarks (model.js:161-170) repeats [...scored].sort(...) and [...out].sort(...) six times. The repeated copy-then-sort is boilerplate that obscures primary vs secondary sorts, and any future sort must remember to spread to stay stable.
proposed_change: Introduce const stableSort = (arr, cmp) => [...arr].sort(cmp); and replace the six inline spread-sorts with it. Pure refactor; identical results and stability.
test_plan: Existing selectBookmarks sort tests in test/model.test.js (recent/top/added primary; folder/added/alpha/domain secondary) fully cover this and must pass unchanged.
rationale: Removes repetition and makes the stability guarantee uniform, with zero ranking change verified by existing comprehensive sort tests.

title: Extract the magic recency horizon (400 days) in rank() into a named constant
problem: rank() at model.js:131 hardcodes 400 * 864e5 as the recency decay window, an unexplained magic number next to the exported YEAR constant. It is untested as a boundary and easy to mis-edit.
proposed_change: Add export const RECENCY_WINDOW = 400 * 864e5; near YEAR and use it in rank(). No numeric change.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js assert a bookmark with lastVisited exactly RECENCY_WINDOW ago yields rec≈0 and one visited NOW yields the max recency contribution, preserving rank order. Existing rank tests still pass.
rationale: Names and pins the ranking horizon so it is testable and self-documenting without altering scores.

title: Share the storage meta KEY constant between store.js and service-worker.js
problem: The literal 'bookmark-ops:meta:v1' is hardcoded independently in service-worker.js:4 and store.js:5. If the schema version is bumped in store.js, the worker will write meta.dead under a stale key and the dashboard will never read it — a silent data-split with no compile-time link.
proposed_change: Export the constant from store.js (export const META_KEY = 'bookmark-ops:meta:v1'; keep internal KEY = META_KEY) and import it in the service worker. Background is type:module so the import is valid.
test_plan: Add an assertion in store.test.js importing META_KEY and asserting it equals 'bookmark-ops:meta:v1' (locks the wire format); the worker test reads/writes using the imported constant.
rationale: Single source of truth removes a drift hazard; resolved key value unchanged. (Merges the two duplicate KEY candidates.)

title: Cover fuzzy streak-reset branch and non-contiguous match scoring
problem: The else streak = 0; branch in fuzzy (model.js:54) is never directly exercised — existing fuzzy tests use contiguous or boundary matches. A query whose characters are non-contiguous in the text is needed to cover the streak reset and confirm hit indices.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change.
test_plan: In describe('fuzzy') add: expect(fuzzy('ac','abc').hits).toEqual(new Set([0,2])); and assert a contiguous match (fuzzy('ab','ab')) scores higher than the spread-out same characters (fuzzy('ab','axb')) because the streak resets.
rationale: Closes a known uncovered branch and locks the streak-bonus ranking that drives result ordering, without touching source.

title: Test urlKey with multiple tracking params and mixed real params
problem: urlKey tracking-param stripping (model.js:35) and the searchParams.toString() rebuild are only tested with a single param. The forEach over interleaved tracking + real params, and stability of the rebuilt multi-param query string, are uncovered.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change (run AFTER OPT-001 so the anchored regex is locked).
test_plan: Add: urlKey('https://x.com/p?utm_source=a&utm_medium=b&gclid=c&q=1')==='x.com/p?q=1'; urlKey('https://x.com/?fbclid=z')==='x.com/'; urlKey('https://x.com/?gclid=1&id=2')==='x.com/?id=2'; and a stability check urlKey('https://e.com/p?b=2&a=1')===urlKey('https://e.com/p?b=2&a=1').
rationale: Hardens the canonical-key contract that drives duplicate detection app-wide. (Merges the two urlKey multi-param test candidates.)

title: Test selectBookmarks >N visits excludes undefined visitCount and is: operator overrides scope
problem: Two selectBookmarks branches are untested: minVisits compares b.visitCount >= ops.minVisits (model.js:157) which drops items with undefined visitCount; and ops.is (line 150) takes precedence over scope-derived is, so 'is:dead' in the query filters even when scope='all'. Existing tests only drive is: via scope.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change.
test_plan: (1) library with one bookmark lacking visitCount and one with visitCount 5; query '>2 visits' returns only the visitCount-5 id. (2) scope:'all' with query 'is:dead' returns only the dead bookmark, proving the ops.is path filters.
rationale: Confirms two distinct paths into the same filter and the documented numeric-comparison behavior, hardening the query engine without altering it.

title: Cover the equal-_ts tie-break and sync-enabled-but-no-synced-copy branches in getMeta
problem: store.js:40 uses strict > for synced._ts > local._ts, so on identical timestamps local wins — an undocumented, untested tie-break that a refactor to >= would silently flip. Also untested: sync enabled but synced is null (first device, sync key absent) where only local exists.
proposed_change: No code change; add a one-line comment at store.js:40 noting ties favor local, and add two regression tests.
test_plan: In test/store.test.js getMeta describe: (1) setSyncEnabled(true); local _ts:10 + sync _ts:10 -> getMeta().notes equals local. (2) setSyncEnabled(true); local present, syncArea empty -> getMeta() returns the local copy.
rationale: Locks deterministic conflict resolution and the never-synced fallback without changing behavior. (Merges two getMeta-branch test candidates.)

title: Test getMeta returns DEFAULT clone when both storage areas are empty or throw
problem: getMeta (store.js:34-42) falls back to {...DEFAULT, ...(chosen||{})} when readArea returns null, and readArea swallows storage exceptions returning null (line 31). Neither the empty-store fallback nor the throw-resilience is asserted; current tests always pre-seed localArea[KEY].
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change.
test_plan: (1) with empty localArea/syncArea, getMeta() deep-equals DEFAULT. (2) make chrome.storage.local.get throw; getMeta() still resolves to DEFAULT (readArea catch path) and does not reject.
rationale: Guarantees sane defaults even when storage is unavailable, locking the error-swallowing contract that prevents a first-run crash.

title: Test patchMeta deep-clone isolation (structuredClone)
problem: patchMeta clones via structuredClone before mutating (store.js:61) so the object returned by a prior getMeta is not aliased. This isolation is untested; a future change to a shallow spread would silently corrupt nested fields (e.g. tags object) without any failing test.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change.
test_plan: Seed localArea[KEY] with { tags: { '1': ['a'] }, _ts: 1 }; capture firstMeta = await getMeta(); run patchMeta(d => d.tags['1'].push('b')); assert the freshly stored meta has tags['1']=['a','b'] while firstMeta.tags['1'] is still ['a'].
rationale: Protects the deep-clone invariant that keeps nested edits from leaking across reads — an aliasing bug invisible until data corrupts.

title: Assert setMeta still writes local when the local set rejects (quota)
problem: store.js:50 wraps the local write in try { } catch { /* quota */ } and still proceeds to the sync mirror and returns { meta, oversize }. The swallow-and-continue behavior on a failing local write is untested; a regression that throws out of setMeta on local-quota failure would break every save path silently.
proposed_change: No code change; add a test where chrome.storage.local.set rejects and assert setMeta resolves and still returns a meta object with _ts stamped.
test_plan: In test/store.test.js, override chrome.storage.local.set to async () => { throw new Error('QUOTA'); }, call setMeta({ pinned: ['a'] }), assert it resolves with meta._ts set and oversize === false (sync disabled).
rationale: Confirms the intended best-effort local-write resilience under quota errors.

title: Test the at-boundary oversize threshold in setMeta
problem: store.js:53 mirrors to sync only when size < SYNC_BUDGET. Existing tests exercise clearly-over (90KB+10) and small payloads; the boundary semantics (< vs >=, i.e. exactly-at-budget is treated as oversize) are untested, so an off-by-one change to the comparator would go unnoticed.
proposed_change: No code change; add boundary tests asserting a payload serializing to exactly SYNC_BUDGET length is treated as oversize (not mirrored) and one just under is mirrored.
test_plan: Construct meta whose JSON.stringify(meta).length is exactly 90*1024 and assert oversize === true and syncArea[KEY] undefined; build one a few bytes under and assert it mirrors. Compute filler length from the serialized envelope so the test is exact.
rationale: Pins the ~90KB cutoff contract that protects against chrome.storage.sync quota rejection.

title: Test store.subscribe filters by KEY/area and unsubscribes
problem: subscribe (store.js:161-165) is exported but untested. Its handler ignores changes for areas other than local/sync and keys other than KEY, calls cb with newValue, and returns an unsubscribe function — none verified.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Replace the no-op onChanged stub with one that captures the registered handler so the test can invoke it.
test_plan: Make addListener capture the handler. Then: (1) handler(KEY change,'local') invokes cb with newValue; (2) handler({otherKey},'local') and handler(KEY change,'managed') do NOT invoke cb; (3) the returned function calls removeListener with the same handler.
rationale: subscribe drives cross-tab live updates; the test locks key/area filtering so an accidental broadening is caught.

title: Test exportHtml ADD_DATE second-rounding and TAGS joining
problem: The exportHtml test checks DOCTYPE, HREF, and escaping but not the ADD_DATE seconds conversion Math.floor(b.dateAdded/1e3) (bookmarks.js:223) nor the comma-joined TAGS attribute.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change (compatible with OPT-003/OPT-004 changes).
test_plan: Extend the exportHtml test: for dateAdded 1700000000000 assert the row contains ADD_DATE="1700000000"; for tags ['x','y'] assert TAGS="x,y".
rationale: Netscape importers rely on ADD_DATE being in seconds; pinning the conversion and tag join keeps exports importable by other browsers.

title: Cover bookmark with missing dateAdded and empty-title host fallback in walk
problem: walk (bookmarks.js:26,28) defaults dateAdded: n.dateAdded || Date.now() and title: n.title || hostOf(n.url). Chrome can return imported/legacy nodes without dateAdded and bookmarks without titles. Neither fallback is exercised in loadEnriched, so downstream sorting/export and title-based search/rank rely on unverified behavior.
proposed_change: No code change; add fixtures with a missing-dateAdded bookmark and a title-less bookmark and assert the fallbacks.
test_plan: In test/bookmarks.test.js (fake timers fixed to NOW): add a node without dateAdded and assert flattened dateAdded === NOW (finite >0); add { id:'104', parentId:'1', title:'', url:'https://www.foo.example/x', dateAdded:5000 } and assert flattened title === 'foo.example'.
rationale: Locks the two core walk fallbacks the UI/ranking/export depend on. (Merges two walk-fallback test candidates.)

title: Cover walk empty-title nested folders and deep (depth-3) nesting
problem: walk (bookmarks.js:36) builds nested paths as path ? path + '/' + name : name; a folder with an empty title at depth>=2 yields a path like 'Dev/'. buildFolderTree (line 134) splits on '/' with .filter(Boolean) so counts land correctly, but no test exercises an empty-named nested folder or depth>=3 nesting.
proposed_change: Add a test (no code change) covering a depth-3 folder chain and an empty-title nested folder, asserting produced folder strings and that buildFolderTree counts/names are unaffected by the empty segment.
test_plan: Extend TREE with Dev > '' (empty title) > leaf and Dev > Sub > Deep > leaf; assert the deep leaf's folder is 'Dev/Sub/Deep' and folderTree counts roll up to Dev.
rationale: Verifies tree-walk behavior on degenerate folder names and deep nesting, hardening against silent path-construction regressions.

title: Test buildFolderTree nested subfolders, empty top-level folder inclusion, container exclusion, alpha sort
problem: buildFolderTree (bookmarks.js:123-138) is only tested shallowly (one folder 'Dev', count 2). Untested: nested descendant-inclusive counts (a child bumps every ancestor, line 131), inclusion of empty top-level folders from folderNodes with count 0 (line 135), CONTAINER exclusion of empty folders, and the alphabetical child sort (line 136).
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Extend TREE to include a nested folder (e.g. Dev/CI with a bookmark) and an empty user folder node.
test_plan: In loadEnriched describe: with a bookmark under 'Dev/CI', folderTree's 'Dev' has count 2 and a child 'CI' with count 1; an empty top-level folder node appears with count 0; children sorted alphabetically; no CONTAINER-named node appears even if empty.
rationale: Folder drill-down counts are user-visible; locking the ancestor-bump and empty-folder rules prevents off-by-one sidebar count regressions.

title: Test importJson restore: creation, URL-dedupe skip, CONTAINER folder routing, and metadata merge
problem: importJson (bookmarks.js:157-178) is entirely untested. Behavior-defining branches: skipping items whose url already exists (line 162), folder-name extraction with CONTAINER rejection (lines 163-164), and the patchMeta merge that dedupes tags via Set union, sets notes only when truthy, and conditionally pushes pinned/readLater ids once (lines 167-174).
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Mock chrome.bookmarks.create to return incrementing ids and stub getMeta/setMeta via the existing chrome.storage mock.
test_plan: describe('importJson'): (1) returns count of newly created bookmarks and skips ones already in the existing Map; (2) a top-level folder equal to a CONTAINER name yields folderName=null while a real folder routes through ensureFolder; (3) resulting meta has tags deduped via Set union, note set only when truthy, pinned/readLater ids pushed only once.
rationale: Locks the round-trip contract with exportJson so a future refactor cannot silently drop tags/notes or double-create bookmarks.

title: Test importHtml: parse, dedupe by existing URLs, 1000-item cap, fallback title
problem: importHtml (bookmarks.js:230-240) is untested. Key branches: returns 0 when no new links (line 233), filters out links already in existingUrls (line 232), slices to 1000 (line 236), falls back to hostOf(a.href) when textContent is empty (line 237). DOMParser is used but the test env is node (vitest.config.js environment: 'node').
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Since the test env is node, gate with a typeof DOMParser check or set the test-file environment to jsdom via a // @vitest-environment jsdom comment; mock chrome.bookmarks.create and ensureFolder's getTree.
test_plan: describe('importHtml'): (1) HTML with two A links where one href is in existingUrls returns 1 and creates only the new one; (2) returns 0 (never calls ensureFolder) when all exist or none; (3) a link with empty text uses the host as title. If DOMParser is unavailable in node, set the jsdom environment for this file.
rationale: Confirms the HTML import contract (dedupe + safe defaults); exportHtml is tested but its inverse is not.

title: Test computeSuggestions one-card-per-domain, /search noise filter, hiddenDomains, and 8-item cap
problem: Uncovered filters in computeSuggestions (bookmarks.js:54-60): the search-result rejection pathname.includes('/search') && search (line 57), hiddenDomains exclusion (line 56), dedupe-by-domain keeping the most-visited URL after a visit-desc sort (lines 58-59), and slice(0,8) (line 60). The current test only proves a single happy-path domain.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Build a history fixture with a same-domain pair (different visit counts), a /search?q= URL, a domain in meta.suggestHidden, and >8 qualifying domains.
test_plan: Assert: (1) a domain appears at most once and the surviving URL is the higher-visit one; (2) a /search?q=x URL is excluded; (3) a domain in meta.suggestHidden never appears; (4) with >8 qualifying domains, suggestions.length === 8 (the 8 highest-visit domains).
rationale: These filters define which suggestions users see; pinning them prevents regressions that spam search pages or resurface hidden domains.

title: Test computeSuggestions similarTo / pathLabel and similarOk suppression
problem: The only suggestion test checks the domain list. The behavior-rich branches inside computeSuggestions (bookmarks.js:62-68) are untested: similarTo is populated when the domain is already bookmarked AND the url key is not in similarOk; pathLabel is shortPath(url) only when similarTo is set; similarOk suppresses similarTo.
proposed_change: Add tests, no source change. Extend HISTORY/TREE so a history entry shares a domain with a saved bookmark but a different path, and add a meta.similarOk entry for one case.
test_plan: In loadEnriched describe: (1) a frequent history URL on a bookmarked domain (different path) yields a suggestion whose similarTo = {title,url} of the saved bookmark with a non-null pathLabel; (2) adding that URL's urlKey to meta.similarOk makes similarTo null and pathLabel null.
rationale: Locks the 'don't nag about a page you said is fine' rule and same-domain disambiguation label, the most product-sensitive part of the suggestion strip.

title: Cover the http URL constructor-throw branch in computeSuggestions search filter
problem: computeSuggestions (bookmarks.js:57) filters out search-result pages with a try/catch that returns false on URL parse failure. The preceding ^https?: regex (line 55) lets through strings like 'http://' that the URL constructor can still reject; the catch->false branch is untested.
proposed_change: No code change; add a history fixture whose URL passes ^https?: but throws in new URL and assert it is excluded from suggestions.
test_plan: In test/bookmarks.test.js add { url:'http://', title:'Broken', visitCount:9 } to HISTORY and assert the returned suggestions domains do not include any entry derived from it.
rationale: Confirms malformed-but-http history entries are safely skipped rather than throwing through the suggestion pipeline. (Merges with the parse-failure documentation candidate.)

title: Export and test the faviconUrl pure builder
problem: Favicon.jsx:6-13 faviconUrl is pure URL/encoding logic (chrome.runtime.getURL + encodeURIComponent + size=64) but is not exported and has zero coverage. Its branchy behavior (returns null when chrome.runtime.getURL is absent, encodes the pageUrl) silently breaks on refactor.
proposed_change: Export faviconUrl (named export) from Favicon.jsx with no other change. The component continues to call it identically.
test_plan: Stub global.chrome = { runtime: { getURL: (p) => 'chrome-extension://x'+p } }; assert faviconUrl('https://a.com/?q=1 2') contains pageUrl= followed by encodeURIComponent('https://a.com/?q=1 2') and &size=64. Then set global.chrome = undefined and assert it returns null; restore chrome.
rationale: Adds coverage to the favicon-URL contract (encoding + null guard) without changing runtime behavior; only adds an export.

title: Extract and unit-test ContextMenu single-letter shortcut matching
problem: ContextMenu.jsx:31-39 matches keyboard shortcuts inline: items.find(i => i && i.key && i.key.toLowerCase() === e.key.toLowerCase()), gated by if (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey || e.altKey) return. This logic (modifier guard + case-insensitive single-key match, skipping 'sep'/header entries) is untested and only reachable through a live keydown listener.
proposed_change: Extract a pure helper matchMenuShortcut(items, { key, metaKey, ctrlKey, altKey }) into model.js returning the matched item or null, and call it from the ContextMenu handler. No behavior change.
test_plan: describe('matchMenuShortcut'): returns the item for a matching letter; returns null when meta/ctrl/alt held; ignores 'sep' strings and {header} entries (no .key); is case-insensitive; returns null when no item declares .key.
rationale: Moves brittle event logic behind a pure, tested function while preserving exact current matching. Also hardens against a non-string e.key (covers the keydown-throw candidate by returning null safely).

title: Deduplicate normalizeUrl/hostOf into the model layer
problem: hostOf is defined three times with divergent fallbacks: bookmarks.js:4 returns the raw url on parse failure, BookmarkModal.jsx:12 returns ''. BookmarkModal.jsx:5-10 also defines a local normalizeUrl. These duplicate, untested copies of core URL logic can drift.
proposed_change: Add exported hostOf(url, fallback = '') and normalizeUrl(url) to model.js, preserving each current behavior by passing the url as fallback where bookmarks.js relies on that. Import them in both call sites. Implementations kept byte-for-byte identical.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js: hostOf strips www and returns hostname for valid URLs; returns the provided fallback (default '') on invalid input; returns the url when called with url as fallback (the bookmarks.js contract). normalizeUrl prepends https:// for bare hosts; rejects non-http(s) schemes (null); returns null for empty/whitespace.
rationale: Collapses near-duplicate helpers into one tested function while keeping both existing fallbacks intact. (Merges two dedup candidates.)

title: Add isHttpUrl helper and unit-test it for scheme validation reuse
problem: Scheme validation (http/https only) is needed in several boundaries (import paths, popup quick-add, favicon, scan) but there is no shared, tested predicate; each site reimplements an inline regex or omits the check entirely.
proposed_change: Add export const isHttpUrl = (url) => /^https?:/i.test(String(url || '')); to model.js (a small pure predicate). This is the building block reused by OPT-035..038; adding it alone is behavior-neutral.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js describe('isHttpUrl'): true for 'https://a', 'http://b'; false for 'chrome://x', 'file:///y', 'javascript:1', '', undefined, null.
rationale: Provides one tested primitive so subsequent scheme-guard hardenings share a single verified rule rather than drifting inline regexes.

title: Reject non-http(s) schemes when importing Netscape HTML bookmarks
problem: importHtml (bookmarks.js:230-240) takes every <a> href from an arbitrary user-supplied HTML file and calls chrome.bookmarks.create with no scheme check. A crafted import file can seed the bookmark bar with javascript:, data:, or file: URLs. The add/edit path already enforces http(s), so this is an inconsistent gap in the same trust boundary.
proposed_change: In the filter on line 232, also require an http(s) protocol using a small isHttp(a.href) guard that returns false on URL parse failure (reuse isHttpUrl from OPT-034). Skips javascript:/data:/file: without changing handling of normal links.
test_plan: Add a test/bookmarks.test.js case feeding HTML with one https link and one javascript:alert(1) link; mock chrome.bookmarks.create/getTree; assert create is called once (https) and never with the javascript: URL, returned count 1. (Pairs with OPT-027.)
rationale: Aligns HTML-import scheme policy with the existing add-bookmark policy without altering behavior for legitimate imports.

title: Reject non-http(s) schemes when importing JSON backups
problem: importJson (bookmarks.js:157-178) iterates data.bookmarks from an arbitrary JSON file and calls createBookmark({ url: it.url }) with no scheme validation (line 165). A tampered backup can create javascript:/data: bookmarks. Same trust-boundary gap as importHtml.
proposed_change: Before the create on line 165, skip entries whose URL isn't http(s) using a tryProtocol helper (try { return new URL(u).protocol; } catch { return ''; }) or isHttpUrl. Keep the existing if (!it.url || existing.has(it.url)) continue; guard.
test_plan: Add a test/bookmarks.test.js case: importJson with bookmarks [{url:'https://ok.com'},{url:'javascript:evil'}]; assert only the https bookmark is created (count 1) and createBookmark is never invoked with the javascript: URL. (Pairs with OPT-026.)
rationale: Closes the JSON-restore injection vector consistently with HTML-import and add-bookmark; no change for valid backups.

title: Always call sendResponse so the dashboard scan callback never hangs
problem: In service-worker.js:22-29 the scan(...).then(...) chain has no .catch. If scan() (or the storage read/write inside the then) rejects, sendResponse is never invoked, so the dashboard callback (App.jsx ~351) never runs, leaving scan.active stuck true (the rescan guard then blocks all future scans for the tab's lifetime).
proposed_change: Guarantee sendResponse({ dead }): add .catch(() => sendResponse({ dead: [] })) to the promise chain (or a try/catch around an async IIFE). Do not change the success payload.
test_plan: In the extracted scan/handler test, stub scan to reject and assert sendResponse is still called once with { dead: [] }. Combine with OPT-040's exported handler so this is unit-testable.
rationale: Hardens the message round-trip against a permanently stuck UI state; preserves the existing success contract.

title: Persist scan results before responding, and handle storage errors
problem: service-worker.js:23-28 fires the chrome.storage.local.get/set as a detached promise (not awaited) and calls sendResponse({ dead }) synchronously after starting it. If get/set rejects the rejection is unhandled, and the response (and worker teardown on idle) can occur before the write commits, so meta.dead may silently not persist.
proposed_change: await the read-modify-write before sendResponse, wrapped in try/catch so a storage failure still responds: try { const r = await chrome.storage.local.get(KEY); const meta = r[KEY] || {}; meta.dead = dead; await chrome.storage.local.set({ [KEY]: meta }); } catch {} sendResponse({ dead });. Happy-path stored data unchanged.
test_plan: In the extracted-handler test, provide a stateful chrome.storage.local mock; assert that after the handler resolves local[KEY].dead equals the scan result, and that a rejecting set() does not throw and still results in sendResponse being called.
rationale: Eliminates an unhandled rejection and a persist-vs-respond race in a worker that can be suspended at any time, without changing stored data shape.

title: Restrict the dead-link scan to http(s) URLs
problem: scan() (service-worker.js:34-53) calls fetch(url, ...) on every URL. Bookmarks can legitimately contain file:, chrome:, javascript:, or data: URLs; fetch() throws for these and the catch (line 46-47) unconditionally pushes them into dead, falsely flagging valid bookmarks as unreachable, while also fetching arbitrary schemes from a privileged context.
proposed_change: Before fetching (inside the slice.map callback), skip non-http(s) URLs via an isScannable(url) helper that parses the protocol and returns false on throw. Such URLs are never fetched and never marked dead.
test_plan: Refactor the scheme guard into an exported isScannable(url) helper and unit-test it returns true for http/https and false for file:/javascript:/data:/chrome:. In the scan test, pass a mixed list with a rejecting fetchFn and assert non-http schemes never reach fetchFn (call count == number of http(s) urls) and aren't in dead.
rationale: Removes false-positive dead flags AND reduces what the privileged worker fetches, with no change to results for normal web bookmarks. (Merges the edge-case and security scan-scheme candidates.)

title: Extract dead-link scan into a pure, injectable function and unit-test it
problem: The batching/timeout/error logic in scan() (service-worker.js:34-53) is the most failure-prone code in the slice (network, AbortController, batching, large URL sets) yet has zero test coverage and is written so fetch cannot be injected, so it can only run inside a real service worker.
proposed_change: Refactor scan() to accept an injected fetch: async function scan(urls, { fetchFn = fetch, batch = 12, timeoutMs = 6000 } = {}) defaulting to today's globals, and export it. The message listener calls scan(msg.urls || []) with defaults so runtime is byte-for-byte identical.
test_plan: Add test/service-worker-scan.test.js importing scan: (a) a resolving fetchFn marks nothing dead; (b) a fetchFn that rejects for a specific URL marks exactly that URL dead; (c) URLs > batch size all get fetched (assert call count) and results aggregate across batches; (d) empty array returns []. Use vi.fn() so no network is touched.
rationale: Pure extraction + DI makes existing behavior testable and regression-proof; defaults reproduce today's call exactly. Enables OPT-037/038/041/042 tests.

title: Clear the abort timeout in a finally so timers don't leak per request
problem: In service-worker.js:42-48 clearTimeout(to) runs only on the fetch success path. On any throw before/without abort, the 6s timer is left pending and later fires ctrl.abort() on an already-settled request. Across a large URL set this leaves many dangling timers keeping the worker alive longer than necessary.
proposed_change: Move clearTimeout(to) into a finally block around the fetch so it always clears regardless of success/throw.
test_plan: In the scan test (OPT-040), use vi.useFakeTimers with a fetchFn that rejects synchronously; after the batch settles assert vi.getTimerCount() === 0.
rationale: Deterministic timer cleanup reduces stray work and helps the MV3 worker idle/suspend promptly; which URLs are dead is unchanged.

title: De-duplicate URLs before scanning
problem: scan() (service-worker.js:34-53) iterates the raw urls array; a library with duplicate URLs issues redundant HEAD requests, and a duplicate dead URL is pushed into dead more than once, so the dashboard's 'N unreachable' count can exceed the number of distinct dead links.
proposed_change: At the top of scan do urls = [...new Set(urls)]; before batching. Output stays a list of dead URLs, now distinct.
test_plan: In the scan test (OPT-040), pass ['https://x','https://x'] with a rejecting fetchFn; assert fetchFn called once and dead === ['https://x'] (length 1).
rationale: Avoids redundant network work and double-counting while preserving the set of reported dead links.

title: Guard the scan message handler against a non-array urls payload
problem: service-worker.js:22 passes msg.urls || [] straight into scan, which calls .length/.slice. A malformed message where urls is a non-array truthy value (object/string) would throw inside scan; combined with the missing .catch this hangs the responder.
proposed_change: Coerce defensively: scan(Array.isArray(msg.urls) ? msg.urls : []). No behavior change for the legitimate caller. Optionally also filter to string entries for OPT-040's sanitizeScanUrls.
test_plan: In the extracted-handler test, dispatch a message with urls: 'oops' (and urls: undefined) and assert scan receives [] / sendResponse is called with { dead: [] } and nothing throws.
rationale: Hardens the message boundary against malformed input without altering the legitimate scan path. (Merges with the sender/payload-validation security candidate.)

title: Cap the number of URLs accepted per scan request
problem: rescan sends every live bookmark URL, and scan() iterates the entire list with 6s-timeout HEAD fetches in batches of 12. A very large library means a long-running, unbounded background fetch storm that can keep the worker alive indefinitely. importHtml already caps at 1000.
proposed_change: Add a defensive cap in scan(): urls = urls.slice(0, 5000); before batching (after dedupe). Bounds worst-case work without affecting typical libraries.
test_plan: In the scan test (OPT-040), pass an input of 6000 distinct strings with a resolving fetchFn and assert fetchFn is called at most 5000 times.
rationale: Bounds resource use for pathological libraries; for normal usage the slice is a no-op so behavior is unchanged.

title: Register an explicit early return for non-scan messages in the worker listener
problem: The onMessage listener (service-worker.js:20-32) returns true only inside the scan branch and implicitly undefined otherwise. Correct today, but the early-return shape is easy to break (e.g. adding another async branch) into a silently-dropped sendResponse, and the non-scan path leaving sendResponse uncalled is intentional but undocumented.
proposed_change: Add an explicit early return: if (msg?.type !== 'scan') return; at the top of the listener, keeping the scan branch and return true as-is. Pure clarity/guard refactor.
test_plan: In the extracted-handler test, dispatch a message with type:'other' and assert the handler returns undefined (not true) and does not call sendResponse or scan.
rationale: Makes the async-response contract explicit and harder to regress without changing how any current message is handled.

title: Add a manifest snapshot/assertion test to lock critical fields
problem: manifest.config.js is build-only and untested; regressions to manifest_version, background.type ('module' is required for the worker's ES imports introduced by OPT-012/OPT-040), permissions, commands, or CSP would only surface at extension load time, not in CI.
proposed_change: Add test/manifest.test.js importing the default export and asserting load-bearing invariants: manifest_version === 3, background.type === 'module', service_worker path === 'src/background/service-worker.js', permissions includes storage/bookmarks/tabs, and content_security_policy.extension_pages contains "script-src 'self'".
test_plan: The test itself is the proof; it runs under the existing vitest node environment (manifest.config.js has no chrome/browser deps, just defineManifest which returns the object).
rationale: Pins the manifest fields the worker depends on (esp. type:'module' for imports) so accidental edits fail fast in CI.

title: URL-encode the bookmark URL passed into the Wayback Machine link
problem: WAYBACK (App.jsx:31) builds https://web.archive.org/web/*/${url} by raw interpolation; the sibling DuckDuckGo action (line 433) correctly uses encodeURIComponent. A dead bookmark URL containing a fragment, query, or spaces produces a malformed/truncated archive URL, so 'Open in Wayback Machine' can navigate to the wrong/empty target.
proposed_change: Encode the URL: const WAYBACK = (url) => `https://web.archive.org/web/*/${encodeURIComponent(url)}`;. Mirrors the existing encoded DuckDuckGo action.
test_plan: Export WAYBACK and add a model-style assertion that WAYBACK('https://x.com/a?b=1#c') contains the percent-encoded form, or document as covered by consistency with line 433.
rationale: Fixes a latent correctness/consistency gap with the adjacent encoded action; no change for simple URLs.

title: App.jsx local meta useState default should use the DEFAULT schema
problem: App.jsx:45 seeds React state with a hand-written subset { tags, trashed, archived, dead, filters, readLater, notes, folderStyles } that omits pinned, similarOk, suggestHidden, and _ts present in store.js DEFAULT. During the brief pre-load render, code reading meta.pinned/meta.similarOk sees undefined instead of []. It is patched once loadEnriched runs, but the initial shape diverges from the single DEFAULT source.
proposed_change: Import DEFAULT from store.js and initialize the state as useState({ ...DEFAULT }). No behavior change after load; only the pre-load placeholder becomes complete.
test_plan: Add a store-level guard test asserting DEFAULT contains pinned/similarOk/suggestHidden so the placeholder cannot silently miss them. The App import is verified by lint/build; existing tests unaffected.
rationale: Makes the in-memory placeholder match the persisted schema's single source of truth, preventing transient undefined-field access.

title: Remove the dead, always-overridden .favicon--img rule in styles.css
problem: .favicon--img is declared twice: styles.css:294 sets background: var(--inset); border: 1px solid var(--line);, and enhancements.css:9 (loaded after) overrides the same selector to background: transparent; border: none;. The styles.css declarations are always overridden and never take effect, inviting a future edit to the wrong rule.
proposed_change: Delete the redundant .favicon--img { background: var(--inset); border: 1px solid var(--line); } block at styles.css:294. Leave the enhancements.css rule as the single source of truth.
test_plan: CSS-only; covered by npm run build (CSS still parses) and visual confirmation that favicons keep transparent background/no border (already the rendered state since enhancements.css wins). Diff shows only the dead block removed.
rationale: Removes a declaration with zero rendered effect today, so visual output is provably unchanged while eliminating a duplicate-selector footgun.

title: Stabilize Palette selection when the filtered list shrinks below the current index
problem: Palette.jsx:49 resets i to 0 only on query change. But flat length also changes when live/actions/pinned/suggestions props update for the same query. If the highlighted index i exceeds the new flat.length-1, flat[i] in the onKey Enter handler is undefined and the if (it) guard makes Enter a silent no-op, and aria-selected highlights nothing. ArrowDown self-heals via Math.min, but Enter before any arrow press does nothing.
proposed_change: Clamp the effective index at use: extract export const clampIndex = (i, len) => Math.min(Math.max(0, i), Math.max(0, len - 1)); in model.js and compute const sel = clampIndex(i, flat.length); use sel for aria-selected, the 'on' class, and the Enter lookup. Minimal, no new state.
test_plan: In test/model.test.js describe('clampIndex'): clamps to len-1 when i too large; returns 0 when len is 0; passes through valid indices; never returns negative. The component wiring is covered by lint/build.
rationale: Fixes a real edge case where Enter does nothing after the result set shrinks, with the decision logic locked by a pure unit test; normal navigation unchanged.
